description_long: This is a standard Western-style toilet with a white ceramic finish, featuring a rounded bowl and an attached cistern at the back. The toilet seat and lid are both raised, revealing a slightly stained inner rim and bowl, indicating some use or age. The cistern is rectangular and sits directly above the bowl, with a flush lever visible on the left side. The base is broad and flat, designed to be securely mounted to the floor. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, and the surfaces show some discoloration and wear. The object is typical of public or residential restrooms in urban environments.
description: A white ceramic floor-mounted toilet with a raised seat and exposed cistern, showing signs of use and a visible flush lever.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open seat, bowl interior, and the cistern directly behind the bowl.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the bowl, the cistern, and the flush lever on the cistern's side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the base, with no visible functional parts.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side profile of the bowl and cistern, with the flush lever not visible.
